title: Patch-4
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:Patch-4.jpg]] |name=Patch-4 |region=[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |sector=[[Cademimu sector/Legends|Cademimu sector]] |system=[[Patch system/Legends|Patch system]] |coordinates=M-6{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} |xyz= |distance= |suns=1[[Star Wars (1977) 59|''Star Wars'' (1977) 59]] |orbited= |position= |moons=M-6 |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class= |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain=*Industrial refuse *Junk |water= |interest= |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies=[[Human/Legends|Humans]] |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|Patch 4, dead ahead!"
"I know, I can smell it already!|Luke Skywalker and Lando Calrissian|}} '''Patch-4''' was a trash-covered [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] that was used to store valuable merchandise by [[Orion Ferret]], an infamous weapons dealer and owner of the ''[[Bazarre]]'' [[Black market/Legends|black market]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Patch-4 became the unlikely home of a tight-knit group of refugees cast out of their previous homes by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. However, this small population was threatened by [[Ceasar]], the massive and deadly [[watchbeast]] Ferret used to guard his wares. ==Description== {{Quote|Now, Lando, you know there has to be some place where people can dump unrecyclable machine parts."
"I know, and machines don't bother me. It's the coffee grounds and bantha tripe I can't take!|Luke Skywalker and Lando Calrissian|}} A [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Patch system/Legends|Patch system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Cademimu sector/Legends|Cademimu sector]],{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} Patch-4 was covered in refuse and garbage, producing a very pungent stench that blanketed the entire planet and resulted in a runny, jelly-like surface. The planet's garbage formed into peaks and valleys, and avalanches of trash could be dangerous to beings on the surface. Much of the trash discarded on Patch-4 was machine parts and [[Starship/Legends|starship]] remains, which stuck out as landmarks from the morass on the surface. Despite its unsanitary situation, however, Patch-4 could support a population of [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]], including [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], a group of so-called "hoboes" carved out a living space in tunnels beneath the surface, constructing underground living quarters and managing to subsist for a long period of time. ==History== {{Quote|In time, we all come here, make friends, decide to stay. And is good home. Little smelly, but is not bad—until crumb-bum Ferret show up!|Serja Kesselrook|}} [[File:Ceasar.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Ceasar emerges to attack from beneath Patch-4's surface.]] By the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Patch-4 became home to a small, tight-knit community of refugees cast out of their homes by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]—these so-called "hoboes" had found their way to Patch-4 after spending time hopping [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] and traveling rough across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. However, their community came under threat sometime around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], when weapons dealer [[Orion Ferret]], owner of the [[Black market/Legends|black market]] [[Space station/Legends|station]] called ''[[Bazarre]]'', began to make use of Patch-4 to store some of his more valuable wares. To this end he introduced a massive [[watchbeast]] called [[Ceasar]] to defend his possessions, and the deadly worm-like creature began to terrorize the local population and drive them to take refuge underneath the trash-covered surface. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leaders [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] visited Patch-4 after brokering a deal for four [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] with Ferret, who had kept the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] concealed amidst the planet's junk. However, Ferret planned to leave the Rebels to Ceasar's mercy, then keep the valuable merchandise for himself once they were [[Death/Legends|dead]]. Calrissian and Skywalker enlisted the aid of the "hoboes" and managed to tame Ceasar with a [[sonic pacifier]], allowing them to secure their starfighters and giving the vagabonds their new home back. ==Behind the scenes== Patch-4 appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 59|''Star Wars'' 59]], written by [[David Michelinie]], illustrated by [[Walter Simonson]] and released in [[1982]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 59|''Star Wars'' (1977) 59]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 59}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Patch-4}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Patch-4 }} [[Category:Cademimu sector locations]] [[Category:Junk planets]] [[Category:Planets in the New Territories]] [[Category:Planets in the Outer Rim Territories]]
